title: exacum quadrangulare
intro: Exacum quadrangulare, also known as the blue exacum, is a flowering plant species in the Gentianaceae family, native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antioxidant. Exacum quadrangulare can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
